items.title: Differentiating a nested polynomial
items.scenario: A model's scalar output is h(x) = (3x + 1)⁵. Let u = 3x + 1 so h = u⁵.
items.explanation: Outer derivative 5u⁴ times inner derivative 3 gives h′(x) = 5(3x+1)⁴·3 = 15(3x+1)⁴—the same pattern used when differentiating composed activation pipelines.

items.title: Two-layer MLP loss gradient
items.scenario: A tiny network: z = wx, a = ReLU(z), L = (a - y)² with w=2, x=1, y=3. Forward: z=2, a=2, L=1.
items.explanation: Chain rule: ∂L/∂w = (∂L/∂a)(∂a/∂z)(∂z/∂w) = 2(a-y)·1·x = 2(2-3)·1 = -2. One backward pass yields the weight update direction.

items.title: Log-softmax cross-entropy block
items.scenario: Classification loss often chains log-softmax and negative log-likelihood: L = -log softmax(z)_y where z are logits.
items.explanation: Frameworks fuse these ops but still apply the chain rule internally; the gradient ∂L/∂z simplifies to (p - one_hot(y))—a classic backprop result from composing log, exp and normalize.
